input_candidates.truth_value: Fig. 1 shows (a) the thermocycling test apparatus with restricted expansion and (b) the elasto-plastic bending test machine ZDM-200PU.

input_candidates.truth_value: The fatigue strength of Fe-Mn-Al-C alloys in the temperature range 523–823 K is stated to be higher than at room temperature, with the maximum at 723 K based on 2×10^7 cycles (from Ref. 4).
Deformation aging in zones of microplastic deformation is mentioned as a time-dependent strengthening mechanism at higher temperatures.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Figure 4 force-deflection diagram for elasto-plastic bending (specimen 50 mm working part): 1st cycle H = 150 mm, Q×10^3 = 30 N; 2nd cycle H = 120 mm, Q×10^3 = 40 N; 12th cycle H = 100 mm, Q×10^3 = 35 N. Work spent on elasto-plastic deformation from cycle to cycle practically did not vary; hysteresis loop is repeated.

input_candidates.truth_value: Fig. 3 presents optical micrographs of the austenitic Fe-Mn-Al-C steel before and after tests, and of martensitic Cr-Ni steel before and after tests, illustrating the thickening of grain boundary κ-phase and the decomposition of martensite.

input_candidates.truth_value: Fig. 4 provides force-deflection diagrams for the bending test, showing that the hysteresis loop repeats and absorbed energy remains nearly constant.
input_candidates.prediction_value: The basic plastic deformation mechanism in austenitic Fe-Mn-Al-C is mechanical twinning; deformation twins act as obstacles to further plastic deformation by sliding and twinning. With increasing deformation, dislocation density sharply increases and localized tangles form where slipping systems cross.

input_candidates.truth_value: Fig. 5 shows the true stress–true strain curve of Fe-Mn-Al-C austenite at a strain rate of 1 mm/min.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Under elasto-plastic cyclic loading, highly strengthened local volumes are created at each cycle, and new sites are continuously formed during cyclic loading, explaining the high endurance and invariant absorbed energy per cycle. Failure occurs when every region of the entire working volume has undergone such strengthening.

input_candidates.truth_value: Fig. 6 shows dislocation structure after 10% and 20% cold working, indicating increased dislocation tangles.
input_candidates.prediction_value: For comparative martensitic Cr-Ni steel (Fe-12Cr-1.5Ni-0.2V-1.8W-0.5Mo-0.15C) tested in similar thermal fatigue conditions, the solid solution decomposes into a ferrite-carbide mixture, the martensitic orientation disappears, and mechanical strength decreases; failure occurred after 9000 cycles.
